item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gives me Coraline vibes I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second that opinion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This remember me SCP-400 but with ants.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish we could upvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I first started reading this my immediate reaction was "this is just 493 with ants," but then the puppeteering kicked in, and I really wasn't expecting the actual mimicry.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation sends humans up for the purpose of dying all the time. I can't imagine why they would squirm over one dog for the purpose of maintaining containment stability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Up to that point the corpse people's activities could just be explained away as the ants mimicking routine behaviors, but it seems exceedingly unlikely that they could know ahead of time how each of the humans would react to a dog dying, which suggests that the ants are at least using the prey's prior knowledge and personalities. At most, though, it could suggest that the people are still conscious and in control, at least for the moment.

If we have to consider the corpse puppets as potential people, then we should consider that they're people in containment that have no real prospects for growth or a normal life. We might expect a living child to get over the death of their pet dog and move on, but this one has nothing to move on to.

Under these circumstances, whether or not I personally agree with it (I don't), I certainly see it as plausible that the site director would approve another dog, if the Foundation is going off this hypothesis. If they are, the ant takeover wouldn't necessarily represent the end of the dog's 'life' either, so it's not quite that bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's with the lolfoundation at the end? That seems uncharacteristically cruel, even for the Foundation. I could understand introducing mice, but unless they expected anything special I highly doubt the Foundation would chuck a dog in there and piss off the Ethics committee. I'm downvoting this for now even though that probably doesn't matter with the ratings as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Others have commented on how downright creepy these things are, and I agree, but I'm upvoting for another reason.

There's a brilliant simplicity to containing these things with a bleach moat. It's not a huge part of the article but it's a small detail that keeps sticking in my head even a week after reading this article. So I'm upvoting for bleach moat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And I thought I hated ants before I read this…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh. Oh no.

+1 although throwing a golden retriever to these horrors makes me want to no vote at the very least!

Also, I feel like you could have included a line about one of the ant people flapping their mouth/tongue about as though trying to speak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … do they think they're humans?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was already freaked out by the ants keeping their victims alive to suffer. It went from bad to worse after that. Noooope. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just love the end bit, the ants are literally like actors so stuck in there roles, which just adds to the creepy factor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's kinda cute at the end with the kid and the dog colonies, but kinda horrifying that they'll willingly send in animals to appease a bunch of crazy parasitic ants.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun fact: In organic chemistry, the Formic Group ( O=C--H based compounds) derives its name from the Latin word for ant, formica, because several species of ants use Formic Acid (O=CH-OH) as the basis of their sting, rather than venom. This group includes the preservative formaldehyde (O=CH2).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like something straight out of a horror movie. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*reads article, slowly reaches for flamethrower*
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It did take a while to get to the "good part," and the beginning could be compressed into less paragraphs. The payoff was good, though, so no-vote. Here's my 2 cents:

1. I felt the order in which the aspects of the anomaly are introduced were perfect, don't change that.

+ 2. Paragraphs
- Paragraphs

SCP-2031 are anomalous colonies of Eciton burchellii (“army ant”) that to date have not been seen outside the active zone of Site-2031-A. Examination of the individuals comprising an instance of SCP-2031 have not provided any consistent genetic or morphological identifiers to distinguish them from non-anomalous Eciton burchellii. Experiments releasing non-anomalous Eciton burchellii colonies into Site-2031-A have shown that the introduced specimens will begin showing anomalous behavior associated with SCP-2031 within 20 to 40 days within the active zone. It is unknown if the transition to SCP-2031 is due to an anomalous effect peculiar to Site-2031-A, exposure to SCP-2031, or is a heretofore undocumented learned behavior. Investigation is ongoing.

Until its anomalous behavior manifests, a SCP-2031 colony will behave normally for Eciton burchellii and other examples of New World army ants. Colonies will contain up to 2 million individuals, containing variety of specialized individuals along with a single queen. They will undergo nomadic phases when the entire colony is mobile and forages for food. When they nest in a non-anomalous fashion they form a “bivouac,” linking each other’s bodies together to form a large ball with a complex internal structure, typically in a burrow or a hollow tree.

The way I'm reading this is that the ants aren't anomalous, the site just makes it so. It would probably be clearer to state that, "Site-2031-A is a farm which causes the species Eciton burchellii (army ants) to manifest anomalous properties (referred to as SCP-2031 or SCP-2031-1, up to you) within 20-40 days. It is unclear whether manifestation of newer instances of SCP-2031/-1 is due to Site-2031-A itself, exposure to pre-existing instances of SCP-2031/-1, or heretofore undocumented learned behavior. Investigation is ongoing."

For the second paragraph, it could be compacted into one sentence and combined with the first paragraph. Since army ant nest sizes, the presence of a Queen, and bivouacs are natural, I don't think you need to go in-depth explaining what they are (Google/Bing exists).

That should make it easier for people to get to the juicy part.

3. Since the following paragraphs are more visual and buildup for the reanimation, you don't need to change them up much in my opinion.

4. Incident log is great.

Hope this is helpful!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It takes a long time to get to the good part (that the army ants are attempting to impersonate the creatures they've killed), and the actual mechanism (or lack thereof) of how the army ants were moving their corpse-puppets broke immersion for me.

item.title: Re: Yikes.
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's talking about cattle…

item.title: Yikes.
item.content:encoded:
Creepy as hell, upvoted.

I'd be curious to see what would happen if the foundation attempted to interview one and how it would talk.

Also, one minor nitpick: You listed one of the species as Bos primigenius taurus. Bos primigenius is the aurochs, the extinct progenitor species, where as Bos taurus primigenius is a breed of modern cattle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I was like 'ant zombies, meh', then I was like 'wait, ant zombies that think they are the people they started as?' and then I upvoted. I feel like it's still missing some kind of … punch. Not sure what. It seems like it could go further, but as it stands it is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like it even more if you offered at least a hint of what the carcass movement really looks like. Is it fluid, just like the original creature, a little shamble-y, like a zombie, or (as I can't help but imagine) more like a time-lapse/stop motion movie of something loaded with maggots?

In any event, skin crawlingly good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was ready to downvote, up until the final part. It's well written, yes, as we've come to expect from you, but it was rather bog-standard. Scary, yes. Unique, not so much. But the last part, where the hosts retain their former personalities, really pulled it out of downvote range, and, hell, into upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's well-written, but the main idea here seems like SCP-by-numbers. It's a generic, deadly, parasitic insect swarm that puppeteers people in a body-horror fashion. It'd make a decent horror movie, I don't doubt, but it isn't fresh enough for me to like it as an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read this no more than fifteen minutes after spraying a colony of masonry ants infesting the garage. Can't decide whether to go look or not…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds pretty interesting. Artificial Intelligence? More like Ant Intelligence amiright? :D
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They, uh… wow. Control of the host organism without necessarily robbing it of its own personality. Definitely a small but notable twist on a classic theme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. The main body of the article is really mediocre. Generic uber-swarm with standard excessively nasty parasitic stuff, obvious invocation of entomophobia, etc. But the stuff with 2031-A is intriguing, and unfortunately follows from the lousy stuff.

So I think I'm going to remain neutral. I don't see how this could be improved, but the good stuff is just good enough to save the bad stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn.

Either that's good ant programming, or individual colonies create a self-aware AI…

We must have an interview log!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 and now I gotta find my bug spray.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nopenopenopenopenopenopenope
I just… Gah. +1.

item.title: I'm back (for a moment)
item.content:encoded: This idea has been sitting on my hard drive for a while. I had a free moment to post it.
